label: Caracas
sub: Capital city; seat of Maduro government and opposition National Assembly

label: Miraflores Palace
sub: Presidential palace — center of Maduro government, site of April 2002 coup and counter-coup

label: Maracaibo
sub: Venezuela's oil capital; site of 1922 Barroso II blowout that announced the oil age

label: Lake Maracaibo
sub: World's largest oil-producing lake; over 15,000 wells drilled since 1922

label: Orinoco Oil Belt
sub: World's largest certified oil reserves (~300B barrels); heavily underdeveloped

label: Cúcuta Border (Colombia)
sub: Busiest Venezuela-Colombia crossing; 2019 aid standoff; main emigration corridor

label: San Antonio del Táchira
sub: Venezuelan border town; Tienditas Bridge crossing; humanitarian corridor

label: Valencia
sub: Venezuela's industrial heartland; major manufacturing and petrochemical hub

label: Barquisimeto
sub: Lara state capital; major center of 2014 and 2017 protest movements

label: San Cristóbal
sub: Táchira state capital; epicenter of 2014 guarimba protests that spread nationwide

label: Ciudad Guayana
sub: Heavy industry complex (CVG, SIDOR steel, Alcasa aluminum); production collapsed under Maduro

label: Maturín
sub: Monagas state capital; gateway to eastern oil fields and the Orinoco Belt

label: Puerto Cabello
sub: Main commercial port; PDVSA oil export terminal; military naval base

label: Maiquetía Airport
sub: Simón Bolívar International Airport; Pérez Jiménez fled here 1958; refugee departures hub

label: El Helicoide (SEBIN)
sub: SEBIN intelligence headquarters and detention center; where political prisoners including Leopoldo López were held

label: La Carlota Air Base
sub: Caracas military air base; site of Guaidó's failed April 30, 2019 military uprising

label: Sabaneta, Barinas
sub: Birthplace of Hugo Chávez (July 28, 1954); pilgrimage site for Chavistas

label: Campo de Carabobo
sub: Site of June 24, 1821 Battle of Carabobo; decisive victory for Venezuelan independence

label: Angel Falls (Salto Ángel)
sub: World's highest uninterrupted waterfall (979m); Canaima National Park, UNESCO World Heritage Site

label: Tienditas Bridge
sub: Colombia-Venezuela border bridge; blocked by military during Feb 2019 humanitarian aid standoff

label: Guri Dam
sub: World's third-largest hydroelectric dam; powers ~70% of Venezuela; infrastructure neglect caused 2019 blackout

label: Ciudad Bolívar (Angostura)
sub: Site of 1819 Congress of Angostura where Gran Colombia was proclaimed; historic Bolívar stronghold
